title: Self-employed persons class - Canada.ca
content:
## Self-employed persons class
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
As of June 4, 2019, all in-Canada visitor record, study permit and work permit applications must be submitted electronically, with some exemptions. See the list of programs that are exempt from the mandatory electronic application requirement .
This section describes how to process applications for permanent residence submitted by applicants in the Self-employed persons class.
A self-employed person is a foreign national who has relevant experience and has the intention and ability to be self-employed in Canada and to make a significant contribution in Canada to one of the following:
- the purchase and management of a farm (for applicants before March 10, 2018)
Note: All applications in the self-employed persons class received on or before May 2, 2017, must be processed using the instructions from OP8 (PDF, 488 KB) . Processing offices should give applicants the opportunity to update their application to meet the requirements of OP8.
All applications in the self-employed persons class received after May 2, 2017, must be processed under the SE PDIs , and officers should no longer use OP8 for SE processing.
- Economic classes: Procedures at the Centralized Intake Office – Paper-based (non-Express Entry) applications
- Determining membership in the class
- Assessing the application against selection criteria
- Admissibility and making a final decision
